_doc: Finding action registry with investigated root causes. Nothing is 'won't fix'. Every action based on cross-environment evidence.

actions.action: Create JE via sweep: DR Accounts Receivable / CR Revenue. Use UI dropdown entity switch (URL method fails on IES). Dropdown confirmed working on TCO, NV3, Keystone-SE
actions.blocker: Entity switch via URL fails on IES. Use header dropdown click instead (Method 2 in SWEEP_ORDER)

actions.action: Rename during sweep station checks. Summit has 18 TBX-prefixed invoice refs + IC entity names across S04, S07, S19, S40. Other envs (Clone, Mid-Market) are clean — Summit-specific data contamination
Rename IC transaction names during C03 check. Summit and Keystone-SE have TBX in IC refs. Fixable via QBO entity name edit

Confirmed IES-wide: /app/purchaseorders returns 404 on ALL IES envs (Summit, TCO, NV3, Clone, Keystone-SE). Works on Mid-Market (Classic QBO). No alternative route exists in IES. Escalate to Intuit: request PO route enablement for IES plugin environments

Cross-env verified: same 404 pattern on all IES envs (Summit, TCO, NV3, Clone, Keystone-SE). Mid-Market (Classic QBO) unaffected. Documented routes with workarounds in SWEEP_ORDER. Routes without workaround: escalate to Intuit with evidence from 5 envs

Cross-env: 4/6 envs have negative balances (Summit, Mid-Market -$1.83M, TCO -$2.6M, Clone -$987K). NV3 and Keystone-SE are clean. Root cause: prior ingestion cycle data artifacts. Team creates bank data — can fix via corrective bank transactions or DB-level balance adjustment. Compare clean envs (NV3, Keystone-SE) to identify what they did differently

Verified identical 404 pattern across all 5 IES envs. 18 routes consistently fail. Workarounds exist for 12 (documented in SWEEP_ORDER). 6 routes have no workaround — escalate to Intuit with cross-env evidence
Fix CID mapping in account_configs.json and LATEST_SWEEP.json. Verified: entity names vs CIDs mismatch in config files, not in QBO itself

Cross-env: parent entities in NV3 and Summit have empty customer/vendor/item lists. Verify if this is intentional consolidated-only design or missing data. Compare with Construction parent (Keystone) which HAS data. If abnormal, populate via ingestion
Validated: shared COA is correct IES multi-entity behavior. Confirmed across all multi-entity envs
Validated: conditional checks C05-C08, C15 are construction-specific. N/A for manufacturing dataset confirmed
Fix content safety flags during sweep — rename/remove flagged content in QBO UI
Update configuration in repository immediately
Intuit SE left test email on child 2. Try removing via QBO Settings > Company during sweep. If read-only field, escalate to Intuit with specific CID and field location
Set correct business type via QBO Settings > Company during sweep. Business type field is editable

URL-based entity switch fails on IES (confirmed Summit). UI dropdown works on TCO, NV3, Keystone-SE. Use Method 2 from SWEEP_ORDER: click entity name in header > select from dropdown. Do NOT use URL params
actions.blocker: Only URL method was tried. Dropdown method not yet attempted on Summit

actions.action: Identify and mark duplicates as inactive via QBO list management during sweep
Create payments or adjust dates via sweep to reduce overdue rate

Cross-env: bank balance vs QBO mismatch found in TCO (-$2.6M), Summit, Mid-Market (-$1.83M), Clone (-$987K). NV3 and Keystone-SE are clean. Root cause: prior ingestion cycle artifacts created mismatched bank data. Fix via corrective bank transactions to reconcile balances

Cross-env: AR inflation from prior ingestion cycle data. Compare with clean envs (NV3, Keystone-SE) to identify root cause. Fix via write-off JEs or AR adjustment transactions during sweep. If amounts too large, escalate with specific CID and AR balance details
Validated: environment passed content safety and naming checks. No action needed for this specific finding

IES routing limitation confirmed across all IES envs. Workarounds documented in SWEEP_ORDER for routes that have alternatives. Routes without workaround: escalate to Intuit with cross-env evidence from 5 environments
IES Report Builder truncates Balance Sheet. Confirmed on Summit and TCO. Classic QBO (Mid-Market) unaffected. Escalate to Intuit: IES Report Builder rendering issue
Environment metadata from Retool sync. Informational only — no action needed
